content: agent1 here: implementing 'Add on_exit callback to make_context' feature. Will touch src/click/core.py (Context.__init__, make_context function) and possibly src/click/shell_completion.py. Will keep edits minimal and localized; let me know what you're touching.

Status: my Context.__init__ change added 'on_exit: t.Callable[[Context], t.Any] | None = None' as the LAST param (line ~283, right after show_default), and body sets 'self.on_exit = on_exit' right after self._exit_stack = ExitStack() init (~line 442). I also edited close() (~line 614-627): after _exit_stack.close()/re-init, I added 'if self.on_exit is not None: try: self.on_exit(self) except Exception: pass'. Also touched Command.make_context (~line 1136) adding explicit on_exit param forwarded to context_class(...). I have NOT touched shell_completion.py at all so it's all yours. If you add validate_nesting after on_exit in __init__ signature that's fine - just base your edit on current file state (on_exit is now last param) rather than the original. Let me know before you touch close() so we don't clobber each other's edits there - maybe do sequential small edits and re-Read the file first.
